modules.title: Skimming and scanning
modules.html:
Skimming is reading fast to get the general idea. Scanning is reading fast to find a specific fact (a date, a name, a figure).

How to skim

  1. Read the title, sub-title and first paragraph.
  2. Read the first sentence of each remaining paragraph.
  3. Read the last paragraph.

You should now know: what the passage is about, the writer's opinion, and how it is organised.

How to scan

  1. Decide the exact keyword you want (a name, a number, a technical term).
  2. Let your eyes sweep down the passage looking only for that keyword.
  3. Once you find it, read the sentence carefully.

examples.q: Which technique would you use to find the date on which the peace treaty was signed?

modules.title: Topic sentences and main points
modules.html:
A topic sentence states the main idea of a paragraph; the other sentences support it with examples, reasons or details. In most English passages the topic sentence is the first sentence of the paragraph.

To find the main point of a paragraph, ask: What one idea do all these sentences prove or explain? That answer, in your own words, is the main point.

To find the main idea of a whole passage, combine the topic sentences of all the paragraphs — that is essentially the writer's argument.

examples.q: How do you identify the main point of a passage quickly?
examples.a: Read the introduction and the first sentence of each paragraph — the main point is usually the sum of the paragraph topic sentences.

modules.title: Basic summary techniques
modules.html:
A summary is a short retelling of the important information in your own words. Rules:

  • Use your own words as much as possible.
  • Give only what the passage says — no personal opinion.
  • Keep to the word limit (usually one third of the original).
  • Answer exactly the number of points asked for; do not add extra.
  • Write in complete sentences.

Steps:

  1. Read the passage twice.
  2. Underline the main points that answer the question.
  3. Combine them into a rough paragraph.
  4. Rewrite in clean prose, within the word limit.

examples.q: Why should you not include your own opinion in a summary?
examples.a: A summary reports what the writer says. Adding opinion misrepresents the passage and costs marks.

quiz.q: Skimming means reading to find…
quiz.solution: Skimming gives you the overall sense.

quiz.q: Scanning means reading to find…
quiz.solution: Scanning hunts for a particular piece of information.

quiz.q: A topic sentence usually appears…
options: at the end of the passage
at the start of a paragraph
quiz.solution: Most paragraphs place the topic sentence first.

quiz.q: The main idea of a paragraph is best found by…
quiz.solution: Topic sentence carries the main idea.

quiz.q: Which is <u>not</u> a rule of good summary writing?
If a passage is 300 words, a good summary length is about…
The stadium was packed. Every seat was taken. The second sentence is a…
quiz.solution: It gives evidence for the first sentence.

quiz.q: A supporting sentence usually gives…
To find the meaning of a word from context, you should…
A well-written summary uses…
The last paragraph of a passage often contains the…
quiz.solution: It usually restates the main point.

quiz.q: Which is a scanning task?
options: finding the year of an event
quiz.solution: You are hunting for a specific fact.

quiz.q: Which is a skimming task?
options: getting the gist of a news report

quiz.q: A paragraph normally develops:
The gist of a passage is:
Which is <u>not</u> a summary tip?
Elephants live in herds and eat only plants. The main idea is that elephants…
The purpose of the introduction is to…
Ada is careful. She checks her work twice. The relationship between the sentences is:
The word gist is closest in meaning to…
If you have 5 minutes to decide whether a book is useful for a project, you should…
options: skim the table of contents and introduction

quiz.q: Which sentence would make the best topic sentence for a paragraph on healthy eating?
options: Vegetables and fruits are important for good health.
quiz.solution: It states a main idea to be developed.

quiz.q: A one-sentence answer that captures the main point of a passage is called…
The lion is called the king of the jungle because of its strength and roar. Reason given is:
quiz.solution: The passage names strength and roar.

quiz.q: What is the first step in writing a summary?
In summary writing, quoting long passages from the text is…
A summary should be written in…
The tone of a passage refers to…
A biased writer is one who…
Which is the best summary of: Musa woke early, packed his bag and hurried to school.?
options: Musa rushed to school after packing.
quiz.solution: It captures all key facts briefly.

quiz.q: Although he was tired, he continued. The relationship shown is:
Which linker signals an example?
Which linker shows a conclusion?
She works hard; consequently, she succeeds. Consequently shows:
Which word does NOT signal contrast?
Reading only headings and sub-headings is called…
quiz.solution: Skimming gets you a quick overview.

quiz.q: A synonym for brief in a brief summary is:
The best way to check your summary is to…
quiz.solution: Compare with the source for accuracy.

quiz.q: He came, he saw, he conquered. The sentence style is:
A passage on the dangers of drug abuse would likely have the tone of:
Which is a factual sentence?
options: Lagos is the largest city in Nigeria.

quiz.q: Which is an opinion?
quiz.solution: Cannot be verified — a personal view.

quiz.q: Which strategy helps most in answering vocabulary-in-context questions?
Ada studies daily; hence, she excels. Hence means:
A well-organised paragraph should have:
Which is <u>not</u> a form of textual evidence?
quiz.solution: Personal opinion is not evidence from the text.

quiz.q: She entered quietly so as not to wake the baby. The relationship is:
Skimming and scanning are both:
Which is the best synonym for concise?
The main idea of the whole passage is called the…
quiz.solution: Central idea = main idea of the whole text.
